div1.entryFree: Respite, subst. delay, time granted: to make you understand this, I crave but four days' r. Meas. IV, 2, 170. ourself . . . after some r. will return to Calais, H6A IV, 1, 170. this All-Souls' day to my fearful soul is the determined r. of my wrongs, R3 V, 1, 19 (== term; cf. the German frist).* the bishop did require a r. H8 II, 4, 177. H8 II, 4, 177
